lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock & Wendy Knoller
[Flashback scene from last week, Monica and Chandler's kitchen, Rachel, Ross, Monica and Chandler are there.]

lines.speaker: Jennifer
lines.text: Previously on Friends...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How did the job stuff go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He offered me one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (gasps) You know what? This calls for a bottle of Israels finest.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The job is in Paris. (we see Ross stare in disbelief)
FADE OUT

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God! Please, somebody say something.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So if you take this job you'll be moving to Paris?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Or facing a bitch of a commute.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, it's huge, and it's scary, and it's... really far, far away from you guys, but this is such an incredible opportunity for me. And I've already talked to them about our situation with Emma, and they said they'll do whatever we need to make us feel comfortable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean, I'll fly back and forth, they'll fly you out... Anything we want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My boss said I might be getting a new lamp in my cubicle. (Monica looks at him and can't really place what he just said)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, we'll work it out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you! Thank you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah! (they hug) You sure this is what you want?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think it is. (Ross looks very sad. Phoebe and Joey enter.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, what's going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I got a really incredible job offer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, great! All right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good for you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's in Paris.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? No, no, no! No, no... no... no, no... No, too much is changing, okay? First, Phoebe getting married (to Phoebe) Congratulations! (pointing to Monica and Chandler)... and then these two move into a stupid house in the stupid suburbs...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, this afternoon you said you'd be supportive...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it comes and goes. I wouldn't trust it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, you guys... this is really, really important to me. And it means a lot if you could try to get on board.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course we can. Congratulations. (they hug, but Joey shakes his head.) Yay! (she gestures Joey to come and join in) Joey...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no. My hugs are reserved for people STAYING IN AMERICA.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (walking towards Joey) Joey, it would mean so...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! No! Get your France-going-arms away from me. (He walks out, and Rachel follows him)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You okay with this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it makes me feel sad, but...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Talking to Ross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, Rachel moving to another country? Not being able to see her every day. How can I be okay with this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, but what are we gonna do? She really needs this job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you think if the Ralph Lauren people offered her her old job back, she would take it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How is that gonna happen?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is this the best way to use one of your three magic wishes?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know. I could talk to her boss. Yeah! I met him at that Christmas party. We really hit it off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You mean the guy who kept calling you Ron?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn't say we were brothers.
OPENING CREDITS
Central Perk. Phoebe's reading a newspaper. Chandler and Monica walk in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Phoebe. Hey, tell me what you think. All right. The house next door to the one that we're buying in Westchester? Just went on the market. I wanna take a look at it, but Chandler doesn't.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We close escrow tomorrow, so seeing another house can only confuse us, and we're easily confused. We're not very bright.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But what if it is better than ours? Should we at least look?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do you think Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think that shirt makes you look like you should work at a Baskin Robbins... Anyway... Hey, isn't Joey's agent Estelle Leonard?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She died.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're kidding!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's terrible!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, last Saturday. Wow! She was the first black man to fly solo across the Atlantic. (Chandler and Monica look puzzled) Oh, wait a minute, I read the wrong one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, she was just an agent.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey's gonna be *so* upset.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know. *He* always wanted to be the first black man to cross the Atlantic.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, we cannot tell Joey about this. He's already flipping out about everything that's changing. This will push him over the edge.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Seriously, you don't think we should tell him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, not for a little while. Let's just give him a few days to get used to everything else.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What if he reads it in the paper?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Unless Snoopy says it to Charlie Brown, I think we're okay.
Ralph Lauren. Mr Zelner's office. Ross knocks on the door and enters.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'm a friend of Rachel Green's. Uhm, actually we met at the Christmas party about two years ago.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Oh right, uhm, Don?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Close. Ron. (shakes hands)

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Uh... What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm, well... I'm here to see if you'll give Rachel her job back.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Ah, did she ask you to come here and do this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no. At first I have to get you to agree. Then we'll see if she wants to come back.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Wow, that is tempting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, she loved her job here. And let's face it: you're not gonna find anyone who did it as well as she did it. Isn't that true?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: She is good!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (surprised) Huh, I took a shot there.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: But I'm sorry. There's nothing I can do... Ah, it's not true, there is... nothing I want to do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I see... Thanks very much. (he gets up and walks to the door. On his way out he looks at the photographs Mr Zelner has near his door. He picks one up.) Is this your son?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Yeah, his name is Ross. (Ross looks very surprised) What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, nothing, it's just, it's close to Ron. Does he.. Does little Ross like dinosaurs by any chance?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Yeah, they're all he talks about, why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How would he like to come with me to the Museum of Natural History after everyone else has left, just the two of us, and he can touch anything he wants. (Mr. Zellner looks shocked). I just heard it as you must have heard it and that's not good. Let me start again. I'm a paleontologist, you'll be there with us and the touching refers only to bones... fossils!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: You can really arrange that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You let Rachel come back, and it's done.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Well, I guess having Rachel back wouldn't be the worst thing in the world.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Yes! Thank you! This is great. Thank you so much. And I swear, your kid is going to have the time of his life.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: That's great. I worry about little Ross. He's always reading, he's collecting rocks and he's obsessed with dinosaurs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He'll be fine.
Central Perk. Phoebe's reading, Joey has just entered the room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. Everything ok?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm just mad at my agent.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Estelle? Why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There's a part in a TV movie that I would be perfect for and I didn't even be put up for it! She'd better have a good reason.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm guessing she does.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well (taking his cell phone out of a pocket) I'm wanna hear it, because she keeps doing this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, no, no, wait, wait, wait. All right, I gotta go. Just listen. Promise me, that you will wait a minute before you call her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok. Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because a promise between friends means never having to give a reason. (she leaves)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I love that saying!
Phoebe is outside, taking her cell phone out of her bag and making a call. Joey is inside, and his mobile phone starts ringing).

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello?
Phoebe (doing Estelle): Joey, it's Estelle.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was just gonna call you! That's weird.

lines.speaker: Phoebe-Estelle
lines.text: It's a little coincidental, but believable. (Joey nods in agreement). Listen, I'm sure you're wondering why I didn't get you an audition for that TV movie.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, actually I am!

lines.speaker: Phoebe-Estelle
lines.text: I guess I dropped the ball there. Whoopsie!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's it? You know, it seems all you do lately is drop the ball.

lines.speaker: Phoebe-Estelle
lines.text: Don't take that tone with me. Who you think you are? Alan Lemond, the first black man to fly solo across the Atlantic?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, look. All I'm saying is that you're my agent, ok? And you're not getting me into any auditions and I'm tired of it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe-Estelle
lines.text: What are you saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm saying that... (pause). This isn't working for me anymore, ok? Estelle, you're fired. Goodbye. (he hangs up the phone).

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Man, tough week for Estelle!
Joey's apartment. Rachel's there and Ross enters the room with a stuffed dinosaur

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Emma left her stuffed t-rex at my house. You know she can't sleep without it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, she's asleep now. Stop forcing that thing on her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you're not gonna believe what happened to me today! Ralph Lauren called, and gave me my job back!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Nooo!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yee. I mean, it was so weirdest thing. They fired me and then out of nowhere they just hire me back! I mean, that place must have been falling apart without me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, you're not going to Paris.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I'm still going.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wh... wh...what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When the Louis Vuitton people found out that Ralph Lauren wanted me back, the offered me more money! Isn't that great?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeeeah! (They high-five)
house next to the one the Bings are moving into. Chandler and Monica knock, a lady opens the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi. We're buying the house next door, and we were wondering if we could just take a look around.

lines.speaker: Lady
lines.text: Oh, sure. I'm showing it to someone else right now, but please, look around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It feels like we're cheating on our house. And if we're gonna cheat, shouldn't it be with like a hot, younger house, that does stuff that our house won't do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ours is so much better! This living room is smaller, the dining room looks like a cave! What a hole!

lines.speaker: Lady
lines.text: So? What do you think?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Love it!

lines.speaker: Lady
lines.text: Well, we already have one offer on it, and I think the lady upstairs is goning to make another one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They could be our neighbors, what are they like?

lines.speaker: Lady
lines.text: Oh, the woman upstairs is very nice. She and her husband have two kids, he's on Wall Street and she...

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh my God!
Chandler and Monica are speechless).
Chandler (to Monica): Sure.
Joey's apartment. He's there and Phoebe comes in.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Joey, want come with me to... are you ok?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I just... I just feel bad about firing Estelle. This is got to be killing her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, that wouldn't kill her. Ordinary embolism might.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. She's got to be taking it hard, I was like her only client. Except for this guy who eats paper. And I'm guessing he eats more money than he makes. Look, I know she's not a great agent, but she did stick with me for ten years. I'm gonna call her and hire her again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no! Don't call her! You wait for her to call you (Joey considers it)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because patience is the road to understanding (she thinks) which ... is the key... to a happy heart.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (impressed) You blow me away.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (picking up her bag) All right, so promise you're gonna wait for her to call you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I promise. And that means, never having to give a reason. (Phoebe stops herself from laughing and leaves)
The house Monica and Chandler are viewing. Janice comes down the stairs.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: What a small world!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And yet I never run into Beyonce!

lines.speaker: Realtor
lines.text: You two know each other?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, we go way back. Before Monica made an honest man out of him, Chandler used to be my little love muffin! (does her irritating laugh). So? Are you guys thinking of getting this house too? Ooh! Are we gonna have a bidding war? I'd better warn you, I'm a toughie (playfully punches Chandler, who tries to get away from her)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (at Janice's punching) Don't, don't! (looks disgusted)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, actually, we're buying the house next door. (Janice gasps)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica and with bulging eyes) Why!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looks confused and scared) I don't know why.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ooh, that decides it then. I was on the fence. But knowing that you two would be our neighbors? Ah! now we have to get it! (Chandler and Monica are utterly shocked) Ellen, we're going to talk numbers. (Grabs Ellen by her elbow and pulls her outside)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This can not be happening!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, the realtor said another couple made an offer. Maybe the Janice's won't get it! Maybe the other couple will.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The only way that that is going to happen, is if the other couple are the Hitlers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Thinks a little more) Okay, Okay, (clapping her hands) All right. What if we got both houses? Huh? We can turn this house into a guest house.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That is a great idea! And by the way, I don't mean to sound distasteful, but when did you start crapping money!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, you come up with an idea.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright alright, we still have three hours till escrow closes on our house. We can still get out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But we love our house.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Will we love it so much with her next door? And she's gonna be louder out here too. Just the crickets and (apes Janice's voice) "Oh My God"!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, but if we don't get this house, she's stil gonna show up wherever we go! I mean, at least if she's here, it eliminates the element of suprise. I mean, never again will you have to hear the three words that make your balls jump back up inside your body. (She shows this with her index finger, mimicking it pushing something up)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looks afraid, but at the same time, knows she's right) Well, we have to do something. We can't have her living next door. (Janice's laughs loudly outside) Oh, that does it too. (Motions with his index finger like Monica did)
Mr. Zellner's office. There is a knock on the door.
r Zelner: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (enters) It's me. Ron. (Mr. Zellner looks annoyed) Look, I um, I now Rachel turned you down but I think there is a way you might be able to get her to come back.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: This may surprise you, but re-hiring fired employees, is not my main job.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just hear me out. How would you feel about offering her a raise? (looks hopeful)

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: (obviously amused) Not good, Ron.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Perhaps I can persuade you. What if you can give your son this (Takes a huge egg out of his back) genuine pterodactyl egg (whispers) replica.

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: (Takes a long look at the egg while he considers it) Wow, that's pretty cool (Takes the egg from Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh? So? We have a deal?

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: Okay, you got it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes! yes!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: This is gonna make me very popular.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, (grins) Believe me, the ladies, they love it!

lines.speaker: Mr Zelner
lines.text: (looks at Ross, for a long moment, confused) I meant with my son.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good, 'cause the ladies, not so much. (shakes his head, give him a thumbs up and leaves)
Joey's apartment. He walks in reading his mail, and plays back his messages on the answering machine

lines.speaker: Answering machine
lines.text: Joey, this is Al T. Booker, (Looks annoyed that he called him) the guy who eats paper. I'm sure you've heard that Estelle passed away. (Looks shocked) I wanted to let you know there's a memorial for her at the Westside chapel, tomorrow at ten. Hope you can make it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God. (Still in shock when his mobile rings, he picks it up) Hello?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (on the other side of the line, still pretending to be Estelle) Joey, it's Estelle. (Joey's eyes bulge up, he looks afraid)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Estelle?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I wanted to call and tell you that there's no hard feelings for firing me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (still scared) Ok-ay. I just, I can't believe you're calling me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I didn't think I should just drop by...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no! (Starts looking around the room, terrified) Don't drop by, don't drop by!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Anyway, you did what you had to do. I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Can I ask you something? Uhm, what's it like there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Looks around) It's alright. Look kiddo, I gotta go. Good luck with the career. You're gonna be huge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks for everything Estelle. Bye. (Hangs up and reads the phone display) "out of area". Boy, I'll say.
The house next door to Chandler and Monica's new house. Chandler is pacing worriedly through the living room when Janice enters.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Well... I just talked to Sid, we are definitely putting in an offer on the house... a-a-and I'll bet we get it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The Hitlers will be so disappointed.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: All right, I got to run. Tell Monica I say goodbye. And... I'll see you later, neighbor. (Janice laugh)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait! I just want you to know that... I'm so happy you're going to be here.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, me too... (laughs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because... that way... we can pick up where we left off.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I never stopped loving you.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh... my...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah! I want you... I need you... I must have you Janice Litman Goralnik Neihosenstein.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now that you live next door, we can be together every day. Sid and Monica never have to know a thing.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I don't know what to say... I mean, you know, obviously we have this... heat between us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (stunned) Obviously.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: But I love my husband. And I know you love your wife. Now, I don't think we should get this house now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Don't say that. Don't tangle the dream and take it away.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler, one of us has got to be strong.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Although, maybe just... one last moment of weakness... (she kisses Chandler flat on the mouth. Chandler squirms. When she's finished, he looks at her lovingly but uneasily.) Goodbye Chandler Bing. (She leaves)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (speaking as in pain) They're never coming down now.
Ross's apartment. Ross is working on his laptop when Rachel enters with Emma.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are never going to believe what happened to me today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ralph Lauren called again and they offered me more money.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They did?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. It was the weirdest thing. Zelner called me and he said we'll do everything we can to get you back. And that I should thank some Ron... I don't even know what department that guy's in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, what are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I took it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's great! So you're staying in New York!

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: YAY! WHOO! YAY!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're excited, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (hesitant) Ye-ah. Yeah! You know, the money's great. It's certainly the easier choice...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you know, was I looking forward to going to Paris? Sure. You know, was I excited about working in the fashion capital of the world? Ooh, absolutely... Oh...! Yeah, but you know, this is... it's fine. I'm fine going back to a job where I've pretty much gotten everything out of that I possibly can... (she sits down, and Ross who is stunned to hear all this follows her example)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhm, I hadn't no you... I had no idea you were so excited about Paris. Uhm, I mean, you said you were scared.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well yeah, but I mean, it was good scared though, you know? Like when I-moved-to-New-York scared. Or uhm, when I-found-out-I-was-gonna-have-Emma scared... But this is... fine. This is gonna be good. (they both stare around)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (after a long pause) You should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's what you want. You should go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You really think so?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I really do.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But I already told Zelner that I would come back...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (picks up a skull of some carnivore from his side table and puts it in the same bag in which he brought the pterodactyl egg to Zelner) I'm sure he'll understand.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. ALL RIGHT! I'm gonna do it. I'm gonna go to Paris.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I'm going to Paris. Thank you, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, oh! (They hug)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Oh, I'm so happy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then I'm happy too. (They're still hugging - fade out)
COMMERCIAL BREAK
Estelle's memorial service. Joey is giving a speech. Next to him is a blown op photograph of Estelle behind her desk and there's a man standing next to him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you all for coming. We're here today to pay respects to a wonderful agent and a beautiful woman... (Joey looks at the photograph) ..inside. As Estelle's only two clients we would like to say a few words. (Joey looks for his notes. The man next to him is chewing something.) Dude, where's my speech? (the man swallows something and looks at Joey.) That is entertaining. Al Zebooker everybody. (he applauds and Al shows that there's nothing left in his mouth.)
THE END
